name: ci-cd
title: Define the CI/CD pipeline
description: Pick the CI provider, decide what runs on every push vs every PR vs every release, and how production deploys happen (auto vs gated).

starter.summary: Define how code goes from PR → main → production.
starter.issue: Without explicit CI/CD shape, ad-hoc 'deploy from my machine' patterns emerge. Decide the pipeline so test coverage, deploy cadence, and release approvals are consistent from day one.
assumptions: Repo hosting (GitHub / GitLab / Bitbucket) is decided.
constraints: Test suite runtime budget (affects feedback loops).

positions.title: GitHub Actions, deploy on merge to main
pros: Free for OSS / small private repos
cons: Bad commits go straight to prod (mitigate with feature flags / canaries)

positions.title: GitHub Actions, manual prod gate
positions.description: Auto-deploy to staging on main; prod requires manual approval.
pros: Human in the loop for prod

positions.title: PaaS-native (Vercel/Railway preview deploys)
positions.description: Hosting platform handles CI. Every PR gets a preview URL.
cons: Vendor lock-in (you can leave but lose conveniences)

positions.title: Containerized release with promotion (dev → staging → prod)
positions.description: Build once, tag, promote through environments.

implications: Test suite must run in <X minutes (define X) to keep PRs flowing.
Secret management approach in CI is required.
Branch protection rules must match the chosen flow.

notes_for_agent: For most projects with a small team and feature flags, 'auto-deploy from main' is right. If the project handles money, PII, or regulated data, suggest a manual gate before prod. Always ask: what's the desired release cadence?
